Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceedings By Defendants J. Gary Shansby, Shansby Community Property Trust, And Tequila Partida, Llc
Off Calendar Jan-26-2021 Continued To Feb-17-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS J. SHANSBY, SHANSBY COMMUNITY PROPERTY TRUST, AND TEQUILA PARTIDA, LLC's Petition To Compel Arbitration And Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceedings.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants' petition to compel arbitration is continued to February 17, 2021. " 'In determining whether an arbitration agreement applies to a specific dispute, the court may examine only the agreement itself and the complaint filed by the party refusing arbitration.'" (Rice v. Downs (2016) 248 Cal.App.4th 175, 185, quoting Weeks v. Crow (1980) 113 Cal.App.3d 350, 353.) Thus, the petition to compel arbitration should be decided on the basis of the operative pleading. On January 11, 2021, Plaintiff filed a first amended complaint naming an additional party and adding certain allegations. By February 2, Defendants may file a supplemental brief limited to any issues raised by the additional allegations or newly named party. Plaintiff may file a supplemental opposition limited to the same issues by February 8, and Defendants may file a reply by February 11. The action is stayed pending the hearing on the petition to compel arbitration. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1281.4.)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
